question: What happens after I am accepted for an Erasmus exchange?
expected_answer: FRI nominates you to the partner university, and you must apply to the host institution and for Erasmus+ funding.
model_answer: After being selected for an Erasmus exchange, FRI will nominate you to the selected partner institution. The University of Ljubljana will then prepare your Erasmus+ financial support.

retrieved_sources.text:
UL FRI ERASMUS EXCHANGE PROCESS AND REQUIREMENTS

After being selected for an Erasmus exchange, the following steps occur:

1. Nomination:
FRI nominates the student to the selected partner institution.
The University of Ljubljana prepares Erasmus+ financial support.

2. Application to host institution:
The host university sends instructions on how to apply, including:
- required documents
- deadlines
- accommodation information

CONTACT

FRI Erasmus office:
- email: erasmus@fri.uni-lj.si
- office hours: Monday 10:00–11:00 (Zoom or in person)

FINAL THESIS ABROAD

- Possible but not at all institutions
- Requires:
  - mentor at FRI
  - mentor at host institution
- Must be included in Learning Agreement
- ECTS:
  - Bachelor thesis: 6 ECTS
  - Master thesis: 24 ECTS

ERASMUS SCHOLARSHIP

Application:
- via UL portal
- opens by June 1
- must be completed at least 10 days before departure

Payment:
- 80% before mobility
- 20% after completion

question: What is a Learning Agreement?
expected_answer: It is a document that defines which courses you will take abroad and how they will be recognized at FRI.
model_answer: A Learning Agreement defines which courses you will take abroad and how they will be recognized at FRI.

retrieved_sources.text:
Requirements:
- Must be completed BEFORE departure
- Must include course descriptions (links)
- Must be approved by:
  - student
  - FRI coordinator
  - host institution coordinator

Two systems:
- OLA (Online Learning Agreement – standard system)
- FRI platform (if host is not in OLA system)

COURSE SELECTION RULES

The Master's programme covers topics including: machine learning, algorithms and data structures, computer vision, natural language processing, software engineering, and distributed systems.

A PhD programme in Computer and Information Science is also available. PhD students are typically funded through research projects and work closely with faculty supervisors.

3. Erasmus+ grant application:
Students must apply through the UL online system.
Deadline: at least 10 days before mobility starts.
Required documents:
- Letter of Acceptance
- Online Learning Agreement (OLA)
- Proof of enrollment
- Bank account details

LEARNING AGREEMENT (LA / OLA)

The Learning Agreement defines which courses the student will take abroad and how they will be recognized at FRI.

Conditions:
- active student status
- no outstanding obligations to UL
- no disciplinary sanctions

Duration:
- minimum: 2 months
- maximum: 12 months

ADDITIONAL FUNDING

- Students with fewer opportunities: +250 EUR/month
- Travel support available
- Special needs support available

COSTS AT HOST UNIVERSITY

Students do NOT pay:
- tuition
- exams
- lab or library usage

Possible small fees:
- insurance
- student organizations
- materials

LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS

question: How many ECTS do I need to complete during Erasmus?

- General electives: can include language courses
- Mandatory courses: must match content
- Cannot complete mandatory FRI courses remotely during exchange

ECTS REQUIREMENTS

Health insurance is mandatory for all students in Slovenia. EU students can use their European Health Insurance Card (EHIC). Non-EU students must purchase private health insurance, which costs approximately 50-80 EUR per month.

The official language in Slovenia is Slovenian. However, most UL FRI courses at Master level are in English. Many locals, especially younger people, speak English well.

Student Life at UL FRI for International Students

Ljubljana is the capital of Slovenia and a very student-friendly city. The University of Ljubljana has over 40,000 students.

International students can apply for a place in student dormitories (student dorms) managed by the University. The cost is approximately 150-250 EUR per month. Applications for dormitories open in June each year.

- OLS (Online Linguistic Support) is optional

AFTER RETURN

Students must:
1. Submit documents via UL portal
2. Submit documents in Studis (FRI system)

Deadline:
- within 3 months after exchange
- or before end of academic year

HELP AND SUPPORT

If problems arise, students can contact:
- host institution coordinator
- Erasmus mentor
- FRI coordinator
- UL international office
- student tutors

Health insurance is mandatory for all students in Slovenia. EU students can use their European Health Insurance Card (EHIC). Non-EU students must purchase private health insurance, which costs approximately 50-80 EUR per month.

The official language in Slovenia is Slovenian. However, most UL FRI courses at Master level are in English. Many locals, especially younger people, speak English well.

Non-EU students must apply through the University of Ljubljana international office. The application deadline for non-EU students is March 1st each year. EU students may apply until May 15th.

Required documents include: certified copy of your Bachelor's diploma, transcript of records, proof of English language proficiency (IELTS 6.5 or TOEFL 90), a motivation letter, and a copy of your passport.

question: Can I do my thesis during Erasmus?
expected_answer: Yes, but only at some institutions and with both FRI and host mentors.
model_answer: Yes, you can do your thesis abroad during Erasmus. However, it's not possible at all institutions. You will need a mentor at FRI and a mentor at your host institution. Your thesis abroad must also be included in your Learning Agreement. A Bachelor thesis abroad is worth 6 ECTS, and a Master thesis abroad is worth 24 ECTS.

- office hours: Monday 10:00–11:00 (Zoom or in person)

Study Programmes at UL FRI

The Faculty of Computer and Information Science at the University of Ljubljana offers several study programmes.

At Bachelor level, the main programme is Computer and Information Science, taught in Slovenian. There is no English-taught Bachelor programme currently available.

At Master level, the programme Computer Science is fully taught in English and is open to international students. The programme lasts 2 years (4 semesters) and awards 120 ECTS credits.

ADDITIONAL FUNDING

- Travel support available
- Special needs support available

COSTS AT HOST UNIVERSITY

Students do NOT pay:
- tuition
- exams
- lab or library usage

Possible small fees:
- insurance
- student organizations
- materials

LANGUAGE REQUIREMENTS

Health insurance is mandatory for all students in Slovenia. EU students can use their European Health Insurance Card (EHIC). Non-EU students must purchase private health insurance, which costs approximately 50-80 EUR per month.

The official language in Slovenia is Slovenian. However, most UL FRI courses at Master level are in English. Many locals, especially younger people, speak English well.

FINAL THESIS ABROAD

- Possible but not at all institutions
- Requires:
  - mentor at FRI
  - mentor at host institution
- Must be included in Learning Agreement
- ECTS:
  - Bachelor thesis: 6 ECTS
  - Master thesis: 24 ECTS

ERASMUS SCHOLARSHIP

Application:
- via UL portal
- opens by June 1
- must be completed at least 10 days before departure

Payment:
- 80% before mobility
- 20% after completion
